chapter_title: Impregnated, coated, covered or laminated textile fabrics; textile articles of a kind suitable for industrial use
scope_note: Note 1: 'textile fabrics' for Ch.59 purposes = woven fabrics of Ch.50–55 + headings 58.03/58.06, braids of 58.08, knitted fabrics of 60.02–60.06. Does NOT include non-wovens (Ch.56), felt (Ch.56), or knit pile fabrics (60.01 stays there per Ch.60 Note 1(c) exception).

inclusions.description: Textile fabrics coated with gum or amylaceous substances, of a kind used for the outer covers of books or the like; tracing cloth; prepared painting canvas; buckram and similar stiffened textile fabrics of a kind used for hat foundations
Tyre cord fabric of high tenacity yarn of nylon or other polyamides, polyesters or viscose rayon

Textile fabrics impregnated, coated, covered or laminated with plastics, other than those of heading 59.02. Note 2: applies when VISIBLE to naked eye (no account for colour change); product can be bent around 7mm diameter cylinder at 15–30°C without fracturing; textile NOT completely embedded or both-sides-covered by plastics. Note 3: 'laminated' = assembly with plastic sheet/film by any bonding process (visible in cross-section or not)
Linoleum, whether or not cut to shape; floor coverings consisting of a coating or covering applied on a textile backing, whether or not cut to shape

Textile wall coverings. Note 4: products in rolls, width ≥45 cm, for wall/ceiling decoration, with textile surface fixed on backing or impregnated/coated on back for pasting. Excludes flock-on-paper (48.14) and flock-on-textile (generally 59.07)

Rubberised textile fabrics, other than those of heading 59.02. Note 5: (a) fabrics impregnated/coated/laminated with rubber ≤1,500 g/m² OR >1,500 g/m² with >50% textile by weight; (b) fabrics of rubber-coated yarn (56.04); (c) parallel textile yarns agglomerated with rubber. Excludes foam rubber with textile reinforcing → Ch.40; and 58.11

Textile fabrics otherwise impregnated, coated or covered; painted canvas being theatrical scenery, studio back-cloths or the like. Note 6 exclusions: invisible coatings (→ Ch.50–55/58/60), fabrics painted with designs (not theatrical), fabrics with design-bearing partial flock/dust treatment, normal amylaceous dressings, wood veneer on textile backing (44.08), abrasive on textile (68.05), mica on textile (68.14), metal foil on textile (Section XIV/XV)
Textile wicks, woven, plaited or knitted, for lamps, stoves, lighters, candles or the like; incandescent gas mantles and tubular knitted gas mantle fabric therefor, whether or not impregnated
Textile hosepiping and similar textile tubing, with or without lining, armour or accessories of other materials

Transmission or conveyor belts or belting, of textile material, whether or not impregnated, coated, covered or laminated with plastics, or reinforced with metal or other material. Note 7: 59.10 does NOT cover (a) belting <3mm thickness; or (b) rubber-impregnated belts/belting (→ 40.10)

Textile products and articles, for technical uses, specified in Note 8. Note 8 includes: (a)(i) card clothing fabrics, weaving spindle-covering velvet/rubber; (a)(ii) bolting cloth; (a)(iii) filtering/straining cloth for oil presses (textile or human hair); (a)(iv) flat woven multi-warp/weft technical fabrics; (a)(v) metal-reinforced technical fabrics; (a)(vi) cords/braids for industrial packing/lubricating; (b) textile articles for technical purposes (paper-machine felts, gaskets, washers, polishing discs, machinery parts)

exclusions.condition: Plastic-coated/laminated textile where the coating is NOT visible to the naked eye
exclusions.action: Remain in the textile chapter of the base fabric (Ch.50–55, 58, or 60) — Note 2(1). A very thin invisible plastic treatment on a woven fabric does not move it to 59.03. Visibility test is key; colour change from the treatment is disregarded

exclusions.condition: Products where textile fabric is COMPLETELY EMBEDDED in plastics OR coated on BOTH SIDES with plastics visible to naked eye
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 39 (plastics) — Note 2(3). When the textile serves only as internal reinforcement completely surrounded by plastic → Ch.39. Single-side visible coating → 59.03. Both-sides visible coating with textile fully enclosed → Ch.39
keywords: plastic sheet reinforced textile both sides

exclusions.condition: Plates, sheets or strip of CELLULAR plastics combined with textile where textile is MERELY for reinforcing
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 39 — Note 2(5). Foam rubber or cellular plastics with textile backing for reinforcement only → Ch.39 (for plastics foam) or Ch.40 (for rubber foam). The plastic/rubber is the primary material; textile is incidental reinforcement

exclusions.condition: Rubberised textile with cellular rubber combined with textile for REINFORCING only
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 40 (rubber and articles thereof) — Note 5 exclusion. Foam rubber slab with textile backing purely for reinforcement → Ch.40

exclusions.condition: Transmission or conveyor belts impregnated/coated/laminated with RUBBER, or made from rubber-coated yarn
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 40.10 — Note 7(b). Rubber conveyor belts with textile reinforcement → 40.10. Textile conveyor belts with plastics coating (not rubber) → 59.10

exclusions.condition: Conveyor/transmission belts of textile with thickness LESS THAN 3 mm
exclusions.action: Redirect — Note 7(a): 59.10 does NOT cover belting <3mm. Very thin textile belts → classified elsewhere by the appropriate description

classification_rules.rule: Note 2 — Heading 59.03 (plastics-coated textile): 4-part visibility and structure test
classification_rules.description: Heading 59.03 applies to plastics-coated textiles that pass ALL of: (1) VISIBLE: coating/impregnation visible to NAKED EYE (colour change ignored). (2) FLEXIBLE: can be bent around 7mm cylinder at 15–30°C without fracturing. (3) NOT FULLY ENCLOSED: textile NOT completely embedded; and NOT coated on BOTH SIDES visibly (both-sides-visible → Ch.39). (4) NOT DESIGN-BEARING partial coating: partial coating creating design patterns → back to textile chapter. If any of these conditions is NOT met → not 59.03 (either Ch.39, or back to textile chapter, or 59.07 for other coatings).

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: 59.03 (plastics-coated textile) vs 59.06 (rubber-coated textile) vs 59.07 (otherwise coated) vs Ch.39/40 (coating predominates)
classification_rules.description: Three headings for coated textiles, by coating type: 59.03 = PLASTICS coating (PVC, PU, polyethylene — visible, flexible, not fully enclosed). 59.06 = RUBBER coating (natural or synthetic rubber — within weight limits or >50% textile content). 59.07 = ALL OTHER coatings/impregnations (bitumen, oil, metallic coatings, paint — theatrical canvas only), provided they pass the visibility test and are not otherwise excluded by Note 6. If the COATING PREDOMINATES (rigid, fully encloses textile, or textile is merely reinforcement) → Ch.39 (plastics) or Ch.40 (rubber).

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: 59.11 (technical textile articles) — catch-all for industrial textile products not elsewhere specified in Section XI
classification_rules.description: Heading 59.11 is the RESIDUAL heading for TECHNICAL TEXTILE ARTICLES not described in any other Section XI heading. It applies only to goods meeting the SPECIFIC definitions in Note 8: card clothing fabrics, bolting cloth, oil-press filter cloth, multi-layer technical woven fabrics, metal-reinforced technical fabrics, industrial cords/braids, and textile machinery parts (paper-machine felts, gaskets, washers, polishing discs). General industrial textiles without a specific description → Ch.59 residual (59.07 or 59.11) depending on treatment and form. Note 8(b) covers made-up textile machinery articles not in 59.08–59.10.

classification_rules.rule: Note 5 — 59.06 (rubberised fabrics): weight and composition thresholds
classification_rules.description: Rubberised textile fabrics in 59.06 must meet one of: (a) rubber-coated/impregnated fabric ≤1,500 g/m² (most rubberised textiles); OR rubber-coated fabric >1,500 g/m² but textile >50% by weight (heavy rubberised canvas). (b) Fabrics of rubber-coated yarn from 56.04. (c) Parallel yarns agglomerated with rubber (no weight limit). Critical exclusion: cellular/foam rubber + textile merely for reinforcement → Ch.40 (not 59.06). The weight/composition thresholds separate rubberised textiles from rubber products.

classification_rules.rule: Note 4 — 59.05 (textile wall coverings): width and backing requirements
classification_rules.description: Heading 59.05 covers TEXTILE WALL COVERINGS: products in rolls, width ≥45 cm, for wall/ceiling decoration, with textile surface on backing or impregnated/coated back for pasting. Two types excluded from 59.05 and directed elsewhere: (1) Flock/fiber applied DIRECTLY on PAPER backing → heading 48.14 (flock-coated paper). (2) Flock/fibre applied DIRECTLY on TEXTILE backing → generally heading 59.07.
